title: Static Analysis for Code Safety
concept: Running security scanners (like Bandit or SonarQube) against agent code to detect vulnerabilities before commit.

exercises.prompt: What tool can help ensure your code follows consistent styling?
exercises.explain: A linter helps check your code for consistent styling, just like how a security scanner checks for vulnerabilities.

cards.title: Why Static Analysis?
cards.body: Static analysis helps catch potential problems in your code before you run it. This includes finding vulnerabilities that could be exploited maliciously.

cards.title: What is Static Analysis?
cards.body: Static analysis is the process of checking your code for errors and security issues without actually running it. Tools can scan your source code to find vulnerabilities.

cards.title: Finding Cracks in a Wall
cards.body: Think of static analysis like inspecting a wall for cracks. You look closely to find problems before they become big issues.

cards.title: Using Bandit or SonarQube
cards.body: Bandit and SonarQube are tools that scan your codebase. They help identify vulnerabilities like hardcoded passwords or unsafe functions.

cards.title: Static Analysis Eliminates All Bugs
cards.body: Static analysis does not catch every problem. It's like a safety net, helping catch many issues, but not all.

cards.title: Static Analysis vs. Dynamic Analysis
cards.body: Static Analysis looks at the code, while Dynamic Analysis runs the code to find issues. Both are important for safe code!

left.text: Checks code for mistakes without running it.

right.text: Runs the program to find issues.

exercises.prompt: What is a benefit of using security scanners?

options.text: They help find vulnerabilities before committing code
They make your code look pretty

exercises.explain: Security scanners help find vulnerabilities in your code before you commit it, ensuring a safer codebase.

exercises.prompt: Static analysis can find all possible bugs in the code true or false?
exercises.explain: This is false because static analysis can catch many issues, but it won't find every possible bug.

exercises.prompt: Running security scanners helps detect _____ before commit.
exercises.sentence: Running security scanners helps detect vulnerabilities before commit.
exercises.explain: 'Vulnerabilities' fits here since security scanners are designed specifically to find weaknesses in your code.

exercises.prompt: Which tool would you use to detect vulnerabilities in your code before committing?
exercises.explain: Bandit is a security scanner specifically built to find vulnerabilities in your code, making it the right choice.

exercises.prompt: Why should developers run security scanners like SonarQube on their code?

options.text: To boost the performance of the code
To demonstrate coding skills to others
To identify vulnerabilities and improve security
To format the code for readability

exercises.explain: Running security scanners like SonarQube helps identify vulnerabilities in code, which is vital for protecting against security threats.
